title: Polynomial, Rational, and Nonlinear Models · Mastery Check
summary: Compare alternative growth and geometry models for a production process. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a nonlinear model comparison report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.
Fit and critique polynomial models without extrapolating unsupported behavior.
Interpret zeros, holes, asymptotes, and domain restrictions in rational models.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Model Selection for a nonlinear model comparison report.
steps.why: The Model Selection result for a nonlinear model comparison report is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.
